title: centaurium umbellatum subsp. apertum
intro: Centaurium umbellatum subsp. apertum, also known as greater centaury, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the gentian family that is native to Europe and western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating conditions such as digestive issues and respiratory problems. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an expectorant to relieve coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, greater centaury is often combined with other plants to create teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
